payload.description:
**As a** report consumer, **I want** each topic to carry an independent recency signal alongside its memory tier, **so that** I can distinguish a newly-emerging cluster from a stable established one even when both have similar weights.

## Background

The original memory weight stories (plx-9984cc, plx-806719) specified a single dimension: a reinforcement/decay weight (0.0–1.0) mapped to hot/warm/cold tiers. As the implementation evolved, two orthogonal dimensions were recognised and implemented.

---

**Dimension 1 — Memory tier (hot/warm/cold)**

Derived from `days_inactive`: the number of calendar days between the cluster's most-recent member timestamp and the analysis `end_date`. In the full-rebuild path this acts as a recency-driven weight:

| days_inactive | weight | tier |
|---|---|---|
| <= 14 days | 0.8 | hot |
| <= 30 days | 0.5 | warm |
| > 30 days | decays linearly below 0.5 | warm or cold |

*Answers: how fresh is this cluster right now?*

---

**Dimension 2 — Lifecycle tier (new / trending / established)**

Derived from which time windows are represented across **all** of the cluster's member timestamps, using two class-level constants:

- `SHORT_TERM_DAYS = 14`
- `MEDIUM_TERM_DAYS = 30`

Each member's age is bucketed:

| Condition | Boolean flag |
|---|---|
| age <= SHORT_TERM_DAYS | `has_short_term_memory` |
| SHORT_TERM_DAYS < age <= MEDIUM_TERM_DAYS | `has_medium_term_memory` |
| age > MEDIUM_TERM_DAYS | `has_long_term_memory` |

Derived flags:

- `is_new` = short && !medium && !long
- `is_trending` = (short || medium) && !long
- `lifecycle_tier` = "new" | "trending" | "established"

*Answers: how long has this cluster existed in the dataset?*

---

## Why Two Dimensions Matter

Memory tier alone cannot distinguish a freshly-born cluster from a reactivated old one. Lifecycle tier alone cannot distinguish an active cluster from a fading one. Together they form a 2x3 interpretive grid:

| | new | trending | established |
|---|---|---|---|
| **hot** | Just appeared, very active | Building history, very active | Long-running, currently dominant |
| **warm** | Just appeared, moderate activity | Building history, moderate | Long-running, still present |
| **cold** | Just appeared, low activity | Building, fading | Long-running, dying |

---

## Output Fields Added Per Topic

In addition to the existing `memory_weight` and `memory_tier`, each topic in the report output now includes:

- `days_inactive` (int): calendar days from most-recent member to `end_date`
- `has_short_term_memory` (bool): any member within SHORT_TERM_DAYS
- `has_medium_term_memory` (bool): any member in (SHORT_TERM_DAYS, MEDIUM_TERM_DAYS]
- `has_long_term_memory` (bool): any member older than MEDIUM_TERM_DAYS
- `is_new` (bool): short && !medium && !long
- `is_trending` (bool): (short || medium) && !long
- `lifecycle_tier` (str): "new" | "trending" | "established"

## Implementation Location

- `VectorTopicMemory._derive_lifecycle_flags()` in `plexus/reports/blocks/vector_topic_memory.py`
- Constants `SHORT_TERM_DAYS = 14`, `MEDIUM_TERM_DAYS = 30` on the class
- Called per-cluster in the topic assembly loop, receiving member timestamps and `end_date`
- Key files: `plexus/reports/blocks/vector_topic_memory.py`, `plexus/reports/blocks/vector_topic_memory_test.py`
payload.title: Recency indicator as second memory dimension alongside memory tier
